Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to figure out which cases have longer resolution times The user mentioned that cases taking significantly longer than average might indicate performance issues So I will start by calculating the total time each case took from receiving the ticket to closing it

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the event logs each case starts with Receive Ticket and ends with Close Ticket

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 102 Starts at 0805 on 0301 ends at 0915 on 0302 Let me check again From March 1st 0805 to March 2nd 0915 is 25 hours and 10 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 104 Starts 0820 on 0301 ends 0830 on 0302 Let me confirm From 0820 to 0830 the next day is 24 hours and 10 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Starts 0825 on 0301 ends 0930 on 0303 From 0825 on 0301 to 0825 on 0302 is 24 hours Then from 0302 0825 to 0303 0930 is another 25 hours and 5 minutes Total is 49 hours and 5 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the total times are 101 2h15m 102 25h10m 103 1h20m 104 24h10m 105 49h5m

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the average time would be total divided by 5 Adding them up total is 102 hours Divided by 5 cases 20 point 4 hours average

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So cases 102 and 105 are above average and 104 is also slightly above So these three are the ones with longer times

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: So maybe 102 and 105 are the main ones 104 is also a bit over but perhaps not as significant as 105

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Next I need to look for potential root causes The user mentioned factors like escalations long waiting times or delays before investigation and resolution Let us analyze each case timeline

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 101 is quick No escalations All activities are within the same day

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 has an escalation The time between Assign and Escalate is 2 point 5 hours Then from Escalate to Investigate is 2 point 5 hours Then Investigate to Resolve is 19 hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 103 is fast All same day No escalations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Assign to Investigate is 3 point 5 hours Then Investigate to Resolve is 19 hours

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 after Escalate there are 28 hours before the next Investigate Then from that Investigate to Resolve is 19 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Escalations seem to be a common factor in the longer cases 102 and 105

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For case 104 Assign to Investigate is 3 point 5 hours That is longer than 101 and 103 Maybe the agent assigned to 104 took longer to start investigating

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After investigation resolving takes a long time in cases 102 104 105 all three have 19 hours between Investigate and Resolve

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Maybe the resolution step is taking a fixed time after investigation but that seems unlikely Alternatively maybe there is a delay in the resolution process perhaps due to waiting for customer response or another dependency

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If that is the case then the delay is due to customer response time which might not be a process issue but a customer related delay However if the resolution step is taking that long because the agent is not available then it is a performance issue

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Maybe there is a step missing in the event log The events listed are Investigate then Resolve but maybe there is a step like Awaiting Customer Response that is not captured here

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The escalation process might be causing a bottleneck perhaps the Level 2 agent is not available or there is a delay in their processing

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main factors contributing to delays would be escalations leading to longer investigation and resolution times long waiting times between Assign and Investigate and delays between Investigate and Resolve

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Optimize the escalation process Ensure that when a ticket is escalated the receiving team is notified promptly and has the capacity to handle it without delay

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Check if there is a bottleneck in the assignment process Maybe the system assigns tickets to agents who are already busy leading to delays

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The 19 hour delay in cases 102 104 105 might be due to waiting for customer responses If that is the case then the process could be improved by following up with customers more promptly

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Monitor the time between Assign and Investigate for each case and analyze the Investigate to Resolve delay

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Implement a dashboard to track key performance indicators such as average time in each step number of escalations and reasons for escalations

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Consider training or additional resources for Level 1 agents to handle more complex issues without escalation
